# Liquibase Changeset Patterns

All examples use **YAML format** — consistent with Spring Boot config files.
Each changeset lives in its own file under `db/changelog/YYYY/`.

## Add a nullable column (with auto-rollback)

```yaml
databaseChangeLog:
  - changeSet:
      id: 20250320-001-add-avatar-url-to-users
      author: jdupont
      changes:
        - addColumn:
            tableName: users
            columns:
              - column:
                  name: avatar_url
                  type: VARCHAR(500)
      # rollback is auto-generated by Liquibase for addColumn → dropColumn
```

---

## Add a non-nullable column (requires default value first)

Two changesets — never one. Adding NOT NULL without a default fails on non-empty tables.

```yaml
databaseChangeLog:
  # Step 1: add as nullable with a default
  - changeSet:
      id: 20250320-002-add-status-to-tasks
      author: jdupont
      changes:
        - addColumn:
            tableName: tasks
            columns:
              - column:
                  name: status
                  type: VARCHAR(50)
                  defaultValue: PENDING
                  constraints:
                    nullable: true

  # Step 2: populate existing rows, then enforce NOT NULL
  - changeSet:
      id: 20250320-003-set-status-not-null
      author: jdupont
      changes:
        - update:
            tableName: tasks
            columns:
              - column:
                  name: status
                  value: PENDING
            where: status IS NULL
        - addNotNullConstraint:
            tableName: tasks
            columnName: status
            columnDataType: VARCHAR(50)
      rollback:
        - dropNotNullConstraint:
            tableName: tasks
            columnName: status
            columnDataType: VARCHAR(50)
```

---

## Rename a column safely (3-step, zero-downtime)

Renaming directly breaks code that still uses the old name. Use the expand/contract pattern across releases.

```yaml
databaseChangeLog:
  # Release N: add new column, copy data
  - changeSet:
      id: 20250320-004-add-email-address-column
      author: jdupont
      changes:
        - addColumn:
            tableName: users
            columns:
              - column:
                  name: email_address
                  type: VARCHAR(255)

  - changeSet:
      id: 20250320-005-copy-email-to-email-address
      author: jdupont
      changes:
        - sql:
            sql: UPDATE users SET email_address = email WHERE email_address IS NULL
      rollback:
        - sql:
            sql: UPDATE users SET email_address = NULL

  # Release N+1: after old column is no longer used in code, drop it
  - changeSet:
      id: 20250401-001-drop-old-email-column
      author: jdupont
      changes:
        - dropColumn:
            tableName: users
            columnName: email
      rollback:
        - addColumn:
            tableName: users
            columns:
              - column:
                  name: email
                  type: VARCHAR(255)
                  # Rollback restores the column but data may be lost — document this
```

---

## Drop a column safely (2-release process)

Never drop in the same release as removing the code — deploy code first, drop later.

```yaml
databaseChangeLog:
  # Only run this AFTER the code that references the column is deployed
  - changeSet:
      id: 20250401-002-drop-legacy-phone-column
      author: jdupont
      changes:
        - dropColumn:
            tableName: users
            columnName: phone_legacy
      rollback:
        - addColumn:
            tableName: users
            columns:
              - column:
                  name: phone_legacy
                  type: VARCHAR(50)
```

---

## Insert reference/seed data with rollback

```yaml
databaseChangeLog:
  - changeSet:
      id: 20250320-006-insert-default-roles
      author: jdupont
      context: dev,staging,prod
      changes:
        - insert:
            tableName: roles
            columns:
              - column:
                  name: id
                  valueNumeric: 1
              - column:
                  name: name
                  value: ADMIN
        - insert:
            tableName: roles
            columns:
              - column:
                  name: id
                  valueNumeric: 2
              - column:
                  name: name
                  value: USER
      rollback:
        - delete:
            tableName: roles
            where: id IN (1, 2)
```

---

## Add index without table lock (PostgreSQL / MySQL)

```yaml
databaseChangeLog:
  # PostgreSQL — CONCURRENTLY avoids table lock, cannot run in a transaction
  - changeSet:
      id: 20250320-007-add-index-tasks-user-id
      author: jdupont
      dbms: postgresql
      changes:
        - sql:
            sql: CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_user_id ON tasks(user_id)
      rollback:
        - sql:
            sql: DROP INDEX CONCURRENTLY IF EXISTS idx_tasks_user_id

  # MySQL — use standard createIndex (no CONCURRENTLY)
  - changeSet:
      id: 20250320-007-add-index-tasks-user-id
      author: jdupont
      dbms: mysql
      changes:
        - createIndex:
            tableName: tasks
            indexName: idx_tasks_user_id
            columns:
              - column:
                  name: user_id
```

---

## Environment-specific changeset (context)

```yaml
databaseChangeLog:
  # Only runs when Liquibase context includes 'dev'
  - changeSet:
      id: 20250320-008-insert-test-users
      author: jdupont
      context: dev
      changes:
        - insert:
            tableName: users
            columns:
              - column:
                  name: id
                  valueNumeric: 1
              - column:
                  name: email
                  value: dev@example.com
              - column:
                  name: password_hash
                  value: $2a$12$placeholder
      rollback:
        - delete:
            tableName: users
            where: id = 1
```

Activate in `application-dev.yml`:
```yaml
spring:
  liquibase:
    contexts: dev
```

---

## Precondition — make a changeset idempotent

```yaml
databaseChangeLog:
  - changeSet:
      id: 20250320-009-add-archived-column
      author: jdupont
      preConditions:
        - onFail: MARK_RAN
        - not:
          - columnExists:
              tableName: tasks
              columnName: archived
      changes:
        - addColumn:
            tableName: tasks
            columns:
              - column:
                  name: archived
                  type: BOOLEAN
                  defaultValueBoolean: false
                  constraints:
                    nullable: false
```

`onFail` options:
- `MARK_RAN` — skip and record as executed (most common for "already exists" guards)
- `CONTINUE` — skip silently without recording
- `HALT` — stop the migration with an error (default)
- `WARN` — log a warning and continue
